{{c1::Type of health insurance}} has been shown to be the most limiting factor for access to pediatric orthopaedic management in the United States.
If a procedure requires both plastics and orthopaedic surgery teams , how many surgical timeouts are required ? {{c1::Trick question, it depends}}
{{c1::Fasciotomy}} related lawsuits have the highest rate of successful malpractice claims (48% success rate), followed by lawsuits related to {{c1::musculoskeletal infections}} (43%).

When a Workers' Compensation patient recovers after an injury to a point that further restoration of function is no longer anticipated , he or she is said to have reached {{c1::maximum medical improvement (MMI)}}.

cardSnippets: Bone is weakest in response to {{c1::shear::compression/shear}} force and strongest in response to {{c1::compression::compression/shear}} force.

Ligaments are {{c1::viscoelastic}}, which means that their mechanical behavior is time-dependent . Thus, the relationship between stress and strain is not constant and depends on the time of displacement or load .
Titanium alloys are resistant to surface breakdown (aka high corrosion resistance) in vivo because of {{c1::self-passivation}}.
{{c1::Fatigue}} is defined as failure at a submaximal tensile strength level after numerous loading cycles .
{{c1::Creep}} is the tendency of a solid material to move slowly or deform permanently under the influence of stresses.
Squeaking is a known complication of {{c1::ceramic-on-ceramic::what type of}} hip arthroplasty prostheses  and may be associated with catastrophic failure .

cardTitles: {{c1::Power}} is the ability of a study to detect the difference between two interventions if one exists .

{{c1::Incidence}}: number of NEW cases of a disease diagnosed during a specific time period per unit measurement of pop…
cardSnippets: {{c1::Power}} is the ability of a study to detect the difference between two interventions if one exists .
A {{c1::Bonferroni correction}} is a post-hoc statistical correction made to P values when several dependent or independent statistical tests are being performed simultaneously on a single data set .

{{c1::Incidence}}: number of NEW cases of a disease diagnosed during a specific time period per unit measurement of population. {{c1::Prevalence}}: total number of cases of the disease in the population at a given time.

cardSnippets: Most common complication following distal biceps repair is {{c1::injury to LABC}}.
Distal Biceps Repair Two-incision technique: Increased risk of {{c1::synostosis}} and {{c1::heterotopic ossification}} compared to single-incision technique
The {{c1::biceps}} is the primary supinator of the elbow, while the {{c1::brachialis}} is the primary flexor of the elbow.
When repairing distal biceps ruptures , during the volar approach , the {{c1::recurrent radial}} artery is encountered crossing near the biceps tuberosity and is often ligated during the procedure.

Initial treatment for acute posterior shoulder dislocations: {{c1::Acute reduction and immobilization in external rotat…
For patients with chronic posterior shoulder dislocations with a reverse Hill-Sachs lesion may benefit from a {{c1::McL…

{{c1::Stiffness}} is the most common complication after shoulder labral repair.
cardSnippets: The {{c1::posterior band of the IGHL}} is the primary stabilizer of the posterior shoulder in internal rotation .
The Jerk test and the Kim test can be used to assess for {{c1::posterior}} shoulder instability.
Initial treatment for acute posterior shoulder dislocations: {{c1::Acute reduction and immobilization in external rotation for 4-6 weeks}}
For patients with chronic posterior shoulder dislocations with a reverse Hill-Sachs lesion may benefit from a {{c1::McLaughlin procedure}} to transfer the subscapularis .
Open/arthroscopic posterior labral repair has an {{c1::80-85}}% success rate at 5-7 year follow-up.
{{c1::Stiffness}} is the most common complication after shoulder labral repair.

cardSnippets: {{c1::Calcium sulfate}} is the fastest bone graft substitute to resorb .
{{c1::Osteoconductive}} materials act as a structural framework for bone growth .
{{c1::Osteoinductive}} materials contain factors that stimulate bone growth and induction of stem cells down a bone-forming lineage .
{{c1::Osteogenic}} materials directly provide cells that will produce bone including primitive mesenchymal stem cells , osteoblasts , and osteocytes .

In the superficial zone of articular cartilage , there is a {{c1::high::low/high}} concentration of collagen and {{c1::low::low/high}} concentration of proteoglycans .
